s.m. = dhokhā, q.v. s.m. Deceit, deception, delusion; blunder, mistake; disappointment, baulking; doubt, hesitation; alarm, panic; anything that may deceive or mislead, false appearance, a scarecrow; anything imaginary or unreal, a mirage; an object or form indistinctly seen at a distance
more: n. m. S. धूर्त 1. Deception; make-believe ( buttā ). 1. Ek bār tere dho bich āī, ab na āūṅ bas m ṅ . Rus. Once tricked I've been by you, you dont trick me anew. 2. Pā/?/ch rupaē ko achchhī hai, is meṅ zarā dhokā nahīṅ! 3. Ser meṅ panserī kā dhokā . Prov. In every seer he'll cheat you out of five seers. (He is such a cheat!) 2. Blunder ( bhūl , 4). 3. False color or appearance; delusion; mirage. 4. Disappointment; balk. dhokā-dihī, dhoke bāzī , n. f. Fraud; imposture. dhokā denā , v. a. To cheat ( buttā denā , 1). dhokā khānā, dhoke meṅ ānā , 1. To be taken in, etc. 2. To be misled, seduced. Rāh chhoṛ ku-rāh chale, turat dhokā khāë . Prov. From the right path strayed, by guile betrayed. 3. To make a false step; to miss one's footing; to slip. 4. To be deceived by appearances; to take the shadow for the substance. dhoke kī ṭaṭṭī , n. lit . a false screen. See dhokā , 1. Shakleṅ jo dekhtā h…
s. m. (dakh.) 1. See دهوکها dhokhā. 2. Injury.

Chiṛyā maṅgal gātī jāë! kavvā ḍhol bajātā jāë! Boys' rhyme. The kites aloft scream high, their young ones cry, The sparrows chirp close by, crows cawing vie. ḍhol'kiyā , n. m. def: v.t. To wash, cleanse

Yeh dāg to dhoë se bhī nahīṅ chhūṭegā. dhov'an , n. m. The water in which any thing has been washed. dhoī , n. f. Drippings of the poppy juice. dhoī dāl ; Rus. dhovāṅ dāl , n. f. Pulse washed and husked. Dhomāṅ dā' mūṅg kī khāë Turat boltā khush ho jāë . Rus. Mūṅ/?/ dāl well washed and cooked and eaten, My soul enjoyment fills most keen.

def: v. a. S. ऊढ To remove; transport (اتَهانا 3, 4.). 1. Maiṅ ḍhoūṅgī gārā māṭī, taun ek rāj bulā, balmā! song. The mortar I will carry, a mason, husband, bring! 2. Kīlī par khetī kare, aur pair meṅ de de āg, Rās ḍhoë ghar meṅ dhare, vāṅ rah jāë rākh ! Riddle. Ans. Kumhār potter. Upon a nail he cultivates, the threshing-floor he burns, Cleared from the chaff he houses it, the fire to ashes turns. 3. Ao, sakhī, sab dekhan chālen Rāṅjhe dā chaubārā, Hīr nimānī īṅṭāṅ ḍhoṅdī, Rāṅjhā ḍhoṅdā gārā . Play. Come, girls, and Ranjha's attic building see, come hasten we! Poor Hir brick loads, and Rānjha loads of mortar bearing, see!
adj. & s.m. See ḍhoṅćā.

def: n. m. H. ḍhānā , to demolish. Dilapidated houses; ruins ( khaṅḍar ). In kāche ḍhūṅḍhoṅ māṅ, mulan mo se rahā na jā, balmā! Jo helī chunvāve nāhīṅ, matte hāth uṭhā, balmā . Wom. Rus. song. In broken mud house live I really could not, no, not I, If, husband, you wont build a house for me, hence I will fly.
more: n. f. S. ढुण्ड Search. See talāsh. Uṭh uṭh rī, Ganpat kī mā! bhitar se tū bāhar ā! Ālā ḍhūṅḍ, divālā ḍhūṅḍ, baṛī bahū kā bukchā ḍhūṅḍ! Ḍhūṅḍ ḍhāṅḍ ke āge lā! Pāṅḍe kī paḍhāī lā, Chaṭṭoṅ kī miṭhāī lā, jājkī kā chhallā lā ! Chauk-chaknī, q. v. Up, Ganpat's mother, up! you are within, come out! Search niche and wall, the first wife's bundle just turn out! Search, search and bring them out! the teacher's fee fork out! For pupils sweetmeats bring, the drummer's ring bring out!

more: n. f. A piece of cloth carried round the waist and between the legs, and fastened behind. Bāhman nahāyā kyoṅ nahīṅ, dhoban piṭī kyoṅ? Nisbat . Conundrum. Ans. Dhotī na thī . Why wont the Brahman wash, why is the laundress shoe-beat? 1. He has no dhotī . 2. She wont wash. dhotī-band, dhotī-parshād , n. m. lit . One who wears a dhotī . ( Contemp .) A Hindū, and esp. a Baniyā Woh mugannī na rahā, aur woh sāqī na rahā, Dhotī-bandoṅ ke sivā koī bhī bāqī narahā . Shahr-āshob. The Mutiny of 1857. Āzurdah. more: n. m. S. ध्रुव Pole (of the earth). Dhurū tārā . The polar star.
subs: s.m. (in Myth.) One of the divisions of paradise.

more: n. f. Hedysarum gangeticum , a small tree from the fibres of which bow-strings are made.
n. m. A kind of mainā .

def: n. m. S. धूम Pālī, dhūm , P. dūd . Smoke. 1. Āg bin dhūāṅ kahāṅ? Prov. No smoke without fire. 2. Woh to apne ghar kā dhuāṅ bhī nahīṅ nikalne detī . Wom. She wont even let the smoke out of her house. (She is such a miser). 3. Ek janāvar aisā balī, chhappar toṛ nikal gayā galī . Riddle. Ans. Dhūāṅ smoke. There is a creature oh! so strong! Breaks through the roof and sails along! dhuëṅ uṛā'nā , v. a. 1. See dhajjiyāṅ uṛānā . 2. To impoverish; reduce to poverty. dhuëṅ ke bādal uṛānā , v. a. ( Met .) To make up false stories. dhueṅ bakhernā , v. a. lit . to scatter as smoke. 1. To exhaust one's resources; empty one's larder. 2. To shut up; silence. dhueṅ pānī kā sharīk ,' n. m. lit . A partaker of fire and water. A close or next door neighbour.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀥𑀽𑀫 (dhūma), from Sanskrit धूम (dhūma), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *dʰuHmás, from Proto-Indo-European *dʰuh₂mós, from *dʰewh₂- + *-mós. First attested in c. 1564 as Middle Hindi دھواں (dhvaṉ /⁠dhūā̃⁠/).

more: n. f. S. धूलि Pālī, dhūlī . Dust. See خاک Gaṭhṛī bāṅdhī dhūl kī, rahī pavan se phūl, Gāṅṭh jatan kī khul gaī, ant dhūl kī dhūl . Man. Of dust a bundle made, with air swelled out but just, The skilful knot undone the dust returned to dust. dhūl uṛānā , v. a. 1. To raise or throw dust. Jahāṅ phūl bakhere haiṅ, vahāṅ dhūl kyā uṛāëṅ? Prov. Where I have flowers strewn should I in dust and ashes lie? 2. To wander; roam. Dinoṅ uṛātī maiṅ phirūṅ, bāṭ bāṭ meṅ dhūl, Jab se pī ke des kī gaī ḍagariyā bhūl . Dohā. From day to day, from street to street begrimed with dust I roam, A wanderer since the day I went astray from my love's home. 3. To disgrace or defame. dhūl uṛnā , v. n. See khāk uṛnā. dhūl jhāṛnā , v. n. 1. To dust. 2. To dust one's jacket for him. dhūl-dhoyā , n. m. A washer of refuse or dust. dhūl kī rassī baṭnā , v. a. lit . to twist a rope of sand. To attempt an impossibility; to labor…
s. f. Dust. دهول دهاني dhūl-dhānī, adj. (dakh. i. q. پريشان) Dispersed, troubled, ruined.

ety: Prk. धुणी; S. धुनिः, rt. धु, or धूनिः, rt. धू
def: s.f. Agitation; longing, ardent desire, ardour, passion; ambition; inclination, propensity; diligence, assiduity, application, perseverance; — racking pain in the bones ( = dhunihāʼo; cf. dhunnā).
more: n. f. S. ध्वनि soand. 1. Air; tune. 2. Key-note; leading note. Bhairvīṅ kī dhun aur hai, bihāg kī aur . 2. (S. ध्यान ) Any absorbing thought or image. 1. Rām dhun lāgī, Gopāl dhun lāgī, paise-vāl teri Rām dhun lāgī . Mendicants' cry. In Rām my mind's absorbed, in Gopal absorbed, Oh monied man! for thee in Rām I am absorbed! 2. Apnī dhun meṅ magan hai . He revels in the passion which fills his heart. 3. Le āī hai isko muhabbat kī dhun, Jīyā hai faqat tere milne kī sun . Mīr Hasan. O'erpowering love hath brought him here, To life returned to meet his dear. 4. Betābī dil ke bīch rahī aur khātir ranjāyāt rakhī, Nā kām rakhā mil baiṭhne se, na aur matlab kī bāt rakhī, Ek harf na dāë hoṅṭoṅ par, dhun dekhne ki din rāt rakhī, Jab san-mukh ho gaë dil-bar ke, manzūr yehī ek bāt rakhī, Ṭuk dekh līyā, dil shād kīyā khush-waqt kuë, aur chal nikle Pure love. Nazir. My restlessness locked in my breast…
s. f. 1. ( ध्यान ) Inclination, propensity, application, diligence, perseverance, ardour, ambition, assiduity. 2. (Hind). Pains in the bones. 3. dhun or dhuni (for ध्वनि ) Sound, musical sound.

O loved of God! have a share in my penance! (Give me some wood!) 2. Incense burnt to avert the effects of an evil eye or to purify the air. dhūnī pānī kā sanjog , n. m. lit . meeting of fire and water. 1. Union. 2. Attachment; friendship. dhūnī denā , v. a. To fumigate medicinally, or to expel an evil spirit. Mirchoṅ kī dhūnī do, āp batā degā . Make a smoke of chillies, and the evil spirit will speak. dhūnī ramānā, yā lagānā , v. n. 1. To light a fire after the manner of faqīrs . 2. To become a jogī , 1. q. v.

s. f. 1. Persevering. 2. Smoke. 3. A fire lighted by a Hindū faḳīr, over which he sits, imbibing the smoke, by way of penance. Hence دهوني دينا dhūnī denā, To dun, to importune: to smoke. دهوني لگاني dhūnī lagānī, To insist obstinately, or to persevere in a demand. 4. Fumigation by way of exorcising one possessed; or, as a medical application. دهوني لينا dhūnī lenā, To inhale smoke, or undergo fumigation.

To put in the sun to dry. K pṛoṅ ko dhūp de do. dhūp khenā yā cha hānā , v. a. To burn incense before the altar. dhūp-kāt , n. m. The hot weather. dhūp kī nānī , n. f. lit . the sun's grandmother.
